county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cherokee County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Columbus. Named for the Cherokee people. Rich lead and zinc mining history (Tri-State Mining District with Missouri and Oklahoma). Galena and Baxter Springs were mining towns with diverse immigrant populations.
county_metadata.research_tips: Cherokee County Register of Deeds in Columbus holds land records from 1855. The county was named for the Cherokee people who briefly held land here. Rich lead and zinc mining history (Tri-State Mining District) -- mining company records, worker compensation files, and mine accident reports may be at KSHS. The Cherokee County Historical Society in Columbus has local collections. Galena and Baxter Springs were mining and cattle towns. BLM GLO land patents. Probate Court records from 1855. Italian, Irish, and Eastern European immigrants came for mining work -- check naturalization records at the District Court. Newspapers at KSHS from the 1870s. FamilySearch has microfilmed Cherokee County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Cherokee County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Columbus

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cheyenne County formed 1873, organized 1886. County seat is St. Francis. Named for the Cheyenne people. Far-northwestern Kansas county. Late settlement, primarily through homesteading. Severely affected by the Dust Bowl in the 1930s. Sparsely populated agricultural county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Cheyenne County Register of Deeds in St. Francis holds land records from the 1880s (practical settlement). One of the far-western counties organized in 1873 but not effectively settled until the 1880s. The Cheyenne County Historical Society has local family files. BLM GLO land patents are particularly important as most land was acquired through homestead or timber culture entries. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Catholic and Lutheran church records from German-Russian settlers are at local parishes. The St. Francis Herald newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. KSHS holds some territorial and early county records. Dust Bowl (1930s) caused significant outmigration from this county.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Cheyenne County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in St. Francis

source_profiles.survival_notes: Records begin from practical settlement in the 1873s-1883s

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Clark County formed 1885 from Ford County. County seat is Ashland. Named for Captain Charles F. Clark. Cattle ranching and wheat farming county in south-central Kansas. Meade County borders to the south.
county_metadata.research_tips: Clark County Register of Deeds in Ashland holds land records from 1885. Formed from Ford County, check Ford County records at the Ford County courthouse or KSHS for pre-1885 records. The Clark County Historical Society in Ashland has local collections and cemetery transcriptions. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries. The county was cattle-ranching territory before farming settlement. Probate Court records from 1885. Methodist and Baptist church records from pioneer settlers. The Clark County Clipper newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed some Clark County records. The Ashland area had Mennonite settlers whose church records may be at Bethel College archives in North Newton.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Clark County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Ashland

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Clay County formed 1857 from an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Clay Center. Named for Henry Clay, statesman. Located in the Republican River valley. Fort Riley's proximity influenced settlement. Swedish and German immigrant communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Clay County Register of Deeds in Clay Center holds land records from 1857. The county saw significant settlement along the Republican River valley. Clay County Historical Society in Clay Center has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Swedish and German immigrant communities left church records from the 1860s-1870s -- check Lutheran and Covenant congregations. Probate Court records from 1857. The Clay Center Dispatch newspaper at KSHS from the 1870s. FamilySearch has microfilmed Clay County deed books and probate records. District Court naturalization records document immigrant arrivals. Fort Riley's proximity means military families appear in Clay County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Clay County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Clay Center

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cloud County formed 1860. County seat is Concordia. Named for Colonel William F. Cloud, Civil War officer. Home of the National Orphan Train Complex documenting orphan train riders placed in Kansas families. Nazareth Motherhouse (Sisters of St. Joseph) in Concordia.
county_metadata.research_tips: Cloud County Register of Deeds in Concordia holds land records from the 1860s. Concordia was home to the Brown Grand Theatre and a significant commercial center. Cloud County Historical Society and Museum in Concordia has local collections. BLM GLO land patents. French Canadian, Scandinavian, and German settlers left church records from the 1860s-1870s. Probate Court records from the 1860s. Concordia newspapers at KSHS. The Nazareth Motherhouse archives (Sisters of St. Joseph) in Concordia hold Catholic parish registers and school records. FamilySearch has microfilmed Cloud County records. The National Orphan Train Complex in Concordia documents orphan train riders placed in Cloud County families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Cloud County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Concordia

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Coffey County formed 1855 as an original Kansas Territory county. County seat is Burlington. Named for Asbury M. Coffey, early settler. Located along the Neosho River. John Redmond Reservoir was constructed in the 1960s. Free State connections during territorial period.
county_metadata.research_tips: Coffey County Register of Deeds in Burlington holds land records from 1855. The county was an early settlement area near the Neosho River. Coffey County Historical Society and Museum in Burlington has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Early Methodist, Baptist, and Christian Church records are important for vital events. Probate Court records from 1855. Burlington newspapers at KSHS from the 1860s. FamilySearch has microfilmed Coffey County deed books and probate records. The county had connections to John Brown's Free State activities -- territorial-era records at KSHS. John Redmond Reservoir construction (1960s) displaced some communities; check KSHS for relocation records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Coffey County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Burlington

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Comanche County formed 1885 from Barber and Kiowa counties. County seat is Coldwater. Named for the Comanche people. Sparsely populated ranching and farming county in south-central Kansas. Dust Bowl effects were significant.
county_metadata.research_tips: Comanche County Register of Deeds in Coldwater holds land records from 1885. Formed from Barber and Kiowa counties -- check those county records for pre-1885 entries. The Comanche County Historical Society has local collections. BLM GLO land patents document homestead entries in this sparsely settled county. Probate Court records from 1885. Church records from pioneer Methodist and Baptist congregations. Coldwater newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. The county experienced severe Dust Bowl effects in the 1930s -- population declined significantly. Ranching records may document families; check KSHS manuscript collections.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Comanche County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Coldwater

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Cowley County formed 1867 from Butler County. County seat is Winfield. Named for Lieutenant Matthew Cowley, killed in Civil War. Borders the former Osage Nation reservation (Oklahoma). Arkansas City was a staging point for the 1889 Oklahoma Land Run. Southwestern College in Winfield.
county_metadata.research_tips: Cowley County Register of Deeds in Winfield holds land records from 1867. Winfield and Arkansas City were significant commercial centers near the Oklahoma border. The Cowley County Historical Society in Winfield has extensive local collections. BLM GLO land patents. The county borders the former Osage Nation reservation -- NARA holds Indian Territory allotment records. Arkansas City was a staging point for the 1889 Oklahoma Land Run. Southwestern College (Winfield) archives hold local church records. Probate Court records from 1867. Newspapers at KSHS and Newspapers.com. FamilySearch has microfilmed Cowley County records. District Court naturalization records. Exoduster families settled in the Arkansas City area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Cowley County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Winfield

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Crawford County formed 1867 from Bourbon and Cherokee counties. County seat is Girard. Named for Samuel J. Crawford, Kansas governor. Pittsburg was the center of the Southeast Kansas coal mining district with diverse European immigrant communities. Pittsburg State University. Strong labor union history.
county_metadata.research_tips: Crawford County Register of Deeds in Girard holds land records from 1867. Pittsburg was the center of the Southeast Kansas coal mining district -- mining company records, union records (United Mine Workers), and mine disaster records are essential. Immigrant workers from Italy, Croatia, Austria, Germany, and Mexico left extensive Catholic and ethnic church records. Pittsburg State University's Special Collections hold mining-era documents. The Crawford County Historical Museum in Pittsburg has local genealogical collections. BLM GLO land patents. Probate Court and District Court naturalization records at the courthouse. Newspapers at KSHS. FamilySearch has microfilmed Crawford County records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Crawford County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Girard

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Decatur County formed 1873, organized 1880. County seat is Oberlin. Named for Commodore Stephen Decatur. Site of the Last Indian Raid in Kansas (Northern Cheyenne, 1878). Northwestern Kansas agricultural county. Dust Bowl effects were significant.
county_metadata.research_tips: Decatur County Register of Deeds in Oberlin holds land records from the 1880s (practical settlement). The Last Indian Raid in Kansas (Northern Cheyenne, 1878) occurred in Decatur County -- KSHS has related records. Oberlin was a Dust Bowl community. The Decatur County Museum and Historical Society in Oberlin has local family files and photographs. BLM GLO land patents are critical for tracing original settlers. Probate Court records from the 1880s. Church records from pioneer congregations (Methodist, Catholic). Oberlin Herald newspaper at KSHS. FamilySearch has limited microfilmed records. Czech and German settlers' church records may be at local parishes.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Decatur County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Oberlin

source_profiles.source_name: Decatur County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Decatur County Register of Deeds, Oberlin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Decatur County from 1873; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages; liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records begin from practical settlement in the 1873s-1883s

source_profiles.source_name: Decatur County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Decatur County Probate Court, Oberlin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Decatur County; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters

source_profiles.source_name: Decatur County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1880-1950 for Decatur County; Kansas Territory census 1855 and 1859 may include Decatur County entries at KSHS; state census 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905, 1915, 1925 at KSHS

source_profiles.source_name: Decatur County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (homestead, preemption, timber culture, cash entry) for Decatur County; Kansas is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Decatur County Historic Newspapers -- KSHS
source_profiles.repository: Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Decatur County towns at KSHS on microfilm; legal notices, homestead proof notices, obituaries, marriage announcements, and community news; essential for documenting early settlement in western Kansas

source_profiles.source_name: Decatur County Marriage Records -- Probate Court / KSHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Decatur County Probate Court, Oberlin / Kansas State Historical Society, Topeka
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and records for Decatur County from 1873; Probate Court issued marriage licenses in Kansas; KSHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Kansas marriages; state vital records registration began 1911
